title: Episode #238 Coping with Millennial Grief and Loss with Lia Mancao
podcast_name: The Homecoming Podcast with Dr. Thema
summary: Lia shares her journey with invisible grief and family loss as a Millennial. She and Dr. Thema explore ways of coping and healing with collective losses during difficult times and how to reclaim your wellbeing. Lia Mancao, LCSW, is a licensed therapist, writer, and founder of Alyssa Marie Wellness in Los Angeles. Born in the Philippines and raised in an immigrant household, Lia draws from her cultural roots and lived experience to support adults through grief, identity shifts, relationships, and major life transitions. With over 15 years in the field, she centers self-trust, inner child healing, and emotional honesty in her work. She writes about relationships, self-worth, and the realities of being human, with work featured in outlets like The Knot, PopSugar, MindBodyGreen, and Wondermind. Don't forget to like, comment, subscribe, and share. Mixed & Edited by Next Day Podcast info@nextdaypodcast.com

title: Louise Hay: Your Emotions Can Heal You
summary: This gentle and uplifting message shares timeless wisdom about emotions, self-love, and the power of allowing yourself to feel instead of suppressing what lives inside your heart. It explores how unexpressed emotions can weigh on the body and mind, while compassionate awareness and acceptance open the door to natural healing and inner peace. Through calm reflection and supportive guidance, you are invited to slow down, breathe deeply, and meet your feelings without judgment. ItвАЩs a reminder that emotional honesty is not weakness, but a path toward balance, freedom, and joy. Perfect for quiet moments, morning centering, or emotional reset, this reflection encourages you to reconnect with yourself, nurture your inner world, and choose loving thoughts that restore harmony, one gentle step at a time.

title: Midweek Catchup: Love, Loss and the Things We CanвАЩt Replace
podcast_name: How To Be 60 with Kaye Adams
summary: On this weekвАЩs Midweek Catchup, weвАЩre talking about the strange emotional whiplash of midlife вАФ from ValentineвАЩs Day expectations (or lack of them), to the shock of realising how attached we are to things we swear donвАЩt matter. Kay opens up about losing her mumвАЩs ring вАФ and being blindsided by how deeply it hurts вАФ while Karen reflects on the small, handwritten things youвАЩd be devastated to lose, even though no one else would understand why. Along the way, thereвАЩs chat about ageing bodies, dodgy mattresses, grand-puppies, hotel keycard chaos, and why some weeks just hit harder than others. Whether youвАЩre quietly sentimental, fiercely unsentimental, loved-up, rolling your eyes at ValentineвАЩs Day, or just having one of those weeks, this oneвАЩs a reminder that itвАЩs never just about the thing itself вАФ itвАЩs about what it holds for you. Get in touch with your thoughts at podcast@htb60.com  Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: The Real Reason YouвАЩre Not Losing Fat | With Medical Nutritionist Pete Smith
summary: Pete Smith returns to the O-Studio Podcast for his fifth appearance and this time we go deep on weight management and why so many people struggle to lose fat (and keep it off). We unpack the difference between weight loss vs fat loss, why вАЬcalories in, calories outвАЭ is only part of the story, and how things like thyroid function, hormones, stress/cortisol, insulin resistance, sleep, fasting habits, and even alcohol can quietly block progress, no matter how hard you train. Pete also breaks down what actually works long-term: small calorie reductions without triggering rebound, smarter approaches to fasted training, the role of muscle in fat loss, and why your best starting point is a personal benchmark because everyone responds differently. If youвАЩre heading into a new year wanting a better plan (not another short-term reset), this episode gives you practical, science-backed clarity. CHAPTERS: 00:00 Intro + Pete Smith returns (5th appearance) 01:25 Why weight management is getting worse (NZ stats + modern lifestyle) 06:45 Calories in/out: true, but incomplete (why weight gain isnвАЩt вАЬjust willpowerвАЭ) 08:00 Weight loss vs fat loss (why scales & BMI can mislead) 10:20 The hidden roadblocks: thyroid, hormones, stress/cortisol, insulin resistance 14:10 Fuel hierarchy (alcohol вЖТ glucose вЖТ fat) + why it matters for fat loss 22:10 Keto/carnivore + fasting: benefits, risks, and вАЬorganised variationвАЭ 28:30 Exercise for fat loss vs muscle gain (fasted training + mTOR + protein timing) 35:30 Habits that sabotage progress (snacking/MMC, digestion, consistency) 41:10 Cold plunge & fat loss protocol (shivering, thermogenesis + womenвАЩs considerations) 46:15 Where to start: benchmarks, personalised plan, simple calorie reduction 56:10 Best tracking tools: height-to-waist ratio + metabolism temperature check 01:04:40 Wrap-up + how to contact Pete + outro

title: Desire, Redefined [Luminescence Podcast]
summary: So many of us feel anxious or disconnected from our bodies because we don't understand why our sexual desire fluctuates. This episode breaks down the hormonal dance that drives (and sometimes derails) your sex drive, explaining what happens to your metabolism, mood, and libido through midlife.¬† We get crystal clear on desire vs arousal vs orgasm, why estrogen is a major driver of fantasy and craving, and why progesterone can make you drier, slower to warm up, and harder to orgasm (youвАЩre not broken, youвАЩre hormonally alive). Then we zoom out to the midlife reality: metabolism shifts, insulin resistance, body changes, stress, sleepвА¶ and the underrated longevity superpower that changes everything: connection and community. What youвАЩll learn:   Responsive vs spontaneous desire (both normal)   Why ovulation can feel like вАЬsex brainвАЭ   Why the luteal phase can mean dryness + more foreplay   What shifts in perimenopause (metabolism, muscle, belly fat)   Simple blood sugar levers: protein, fiber, walking, sleep   Why body image and stress can tank libido   Why connection might matter more than any supplement If youвАЩve ever wondered, вАЬIs something wrong with me?вАЭ, this episode is your permission slip: your libido isnвАЩt a personality flaw. ItвАЩs biology + life. Featured Guests: Dr. Jolene Brighten вАУ Naturopathic endocrinologist, author of Is This Normal? and Beyond the Pill Dr. Mariza Snyder вАУ Functional wellness expert, author of The Perimenopause Revolution Rosie Acosta вАУ Host of Radically Loved, yoga and meditation teacher Podcast Partners: LMNTGet a free 8-count Sample Pack of LMNTвАЩs most popular drink mix flavors with any purchase at вБ†DrinkLMNT.com/TLPвБ† Timeline: Go to¬† вБ†Timeline.com/LUMIвБ† and get 30% your first order with code ONECOMMUNE30

title: Tapping into the Unknown. Exploring What We CanвАЩt See. (If you like the Telepathy Tapes, youвАЩll LOVE this). Suzanne Giesemann
summary: Today weвАЩre joined by someone whose life journey challenges everything we think we know about reality, consciousness, and what happens after we die. My guest is Suzanne Giesemann, a former U.S. Navy Commander and aide to the Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of StaffвАФsomeone who once lived firmly in a left-brained, evidence-driven world. After the sudden and tragic loss of her stepdaughter, Suzanne experienced a profound shift that led her to explore the nature of consciousness and the afterlife. What began as personal grief turned into a mission that has since helped millions find healing, meaning, and peace. Today, Suzanne is an evidence-based Medium, Spiritual Teacher, and Author who bridges the gap between science, spirituality, and personal experience. In this conversation, we explore what mediumship really is, how consciousness extends beyond the physical body, and how connecting with loved onesвАФand with a deeper sense of awarenessвАФcan transform grief into growth. Whether youвАЩre a believer, a skeptic, or simply curious, this episode invites you to question reality and open yourself to the possibility that we are far more than we imagine. If you like the Telepathy Tapes, youвАЩll love this conversation. As always, weвАЩre untangle, the podcast from Muse the brain sensing headband designed to train your brain and help you find more focus. Use code untangle 15 to get your discount. title: Seed Oils, Nutrition Confusion & What The Science Actually Says
summary: In this episode of The Fit Positive Podcast, we cut through the noise around seed oils and vegetable oils вАФ and the confusion created by social media narratives вАФ to focus on what science actually tells us about dietary fats. If youвАЩve ever questioned whether certain ingredients are inherently вАЬgoodвАЭ or вАЬbad,вАЭ this episode will help you understand: why seed oils have become a hot topic online how they fit into a healthy diet what researchers like Dr Layne Norton conclude about the evidence how guidance from organisations like the World Health Organization (WHO) frames dietary fat recommendations and why context and patterns matter far more than demonising single ingredients This is a common-sense, evidence-based conversation designed to give you clarity and confidence around your food choices. рЯУМ Key Takeaways вЬФпЄП Social media narratives often lack context:Ingredients like seed oils get labelled without considering overall diet quality, portion size, and the total dietary pattern. вЬФпЄП Seed oils are a source of unsaturated fat:They are one type of dietary fat вАФ energy-dense but not inherently harmful when consumed in normal amounts. вЬФпЄП What science shows:Leading researchers like Dr Layne Norton review the human evidence and find no compelling proof that seed oils are uniquely harmful, toxic, or inflammatory when part of a balanced diet. See References section below for all of the studies that support this finding. вЬФпЄП WHO guidance supports unsaturated fats:The World Health Organization recommends moderating total fat, reducing saturated fats, eliminating trans fats, and prioritising overall diet quality вАФ not avoiding seed oils outright. See links in references section below. вЬФпЄП Macros and thresholds matter:Every nutrient вАФ fat, protein, carbohydrate вАФ has a range where it supports health. Eating within those ranges, and in context of whole diet patterns, is the real key to long-term success. вЬФпЄП All foods can fit in the right quantities:No food or ingredient should be judged in isolation вАФ itвАЩs the bigger picture that determines outcomes. рЯТ° Real-Life Example I eat my homemade nutty granola daily вАФ including the vegetable oil in it вАФ and my bloodwork continues to show excellent metabolic and cardiovascular health. This reinforces the episodeвАЩs central message: no single ingredient overrides an otherwise healthy lifestyle. More on this in my episode on heart health: Episode on Heart Health References & Further Reading: рЯФЧ WHO Healthy Diet Fact Sheet вАФ World Health OrganizationрЯСЙ https://www.who.int/news-room/fact-sheets/detail/healthy-diet?utm_source=chatgpt.com рЯФЧ WHO Guidelines on Saturated and Trans FatsрЯСЙ https://www.who.int/news/item/17-07-2023-who-updates-guidelines-on-fats-and-carbohydrates?utm_source=chatgpt.com рЯФЧ What Are Seed Oils? вАФ Verywell Health (science overview)рЯСЙ https://www.verywellhealth.com/what-are-seed-oils-8742630?utm_source=chatgpt.com рЯФЧ The Seed Oil Debate: Are They Uniquely Harmful Relative to Other Dietary Fats? вАФ The Drive (Layne Norton, Ph.D.)рЯСЙ вБ†https://podwise.ai/dashboard/episodes/6887656?utm_source=chatgpt.comвБ† рЯТЫ Connect With Me рЯУЄ Instagram вАФ @fitpositive.ieвЬЙпЄП Email вАФ pod@fitpositive.ieрЯМР Website вАФ www.fitpositive.ie

title: Unlocking the mystery of Epstein-Barr Virus
summary: Most of us have Epstein-Barr Virus (EBV) hiding in our bodies.  You may know it as the cause of glandular fever or вАЬkissing diseaseвАЭ.  But more research is suggesting that it can turn our immune systems against us to cause autoimmune disease, such as multiple sclerosis or lupus.     So, what is the virus doing in our bodies - and can we do anything about it?  James is joined by Dr Claire Shannon-Lowe, a virologist at the University of Birmingham, and Dr Patrick Kearns from the University of Edinburgh, a neurologist and epidemiologist. And we delve into the world of echolocation.  Bats hunt at night by making pulses of sounds and listening to the echoes so they can build a picture of the world around them in total darkness - and it's a technique some people with visual impairment use to explore the world. A team at the University of East Anglia has been testing how well humans can pick up these skills, so James pays a visit to have a go... Presenter: James Gallagher Producers: Gerry Holt, Tom Bonnett & Thomas Hunt Production coordinator: Stuart Laws Content editor: Ilan Goodman
